BackendProfile.description:
All of ONE backend's featured-case metrics, grouped (no parallel dicts).

Element types match the per-panel charts exactly: `scaling`/`ecdf_*` keep the
(x, y) `Point2D` axes, `param_err` is per-parameter, `param_spread`/`stability`
keep the mean±sd `SpreadPt` bands.

convEff.description: Per-iteration convergence efficiency (cost reduction per step) derived from `conv`. Populated ONLY when `history_source == 'real'` (the subject's measured cost history); `None` for backends whose history is `reconstructed`, since a proxy-derived efficiency must not be presented as a measured series. Additive field — old payloads validate with None.
convEff.title: Conveff

jacobianConditionNumber.description: κ(J) at convergence. Structural property of the case, not the solver. κ ≥ 1e6 → ill-conditioned (explains backend disagreement); κ ≤ 1e4 → well-posed.
jacobianConditionNumber.title: Jacobianconditionnumber

thetaDistance.description: Scale-normalized per-iteration distance of the parameter vector to the synthetic ground truth, dₖ = ‖(θₖ − θ_true)/s‖₂ (s = per-parameter magnitude scale). Same length as `conv`. The REAL convergence-to-truth metric — distinct from the χ² descent in `conv`. None for non-synthetic cases (no θ_true) or backends without a parameter trajectory (only spectrafit's faer LM records it). Additive field — old payloads validate with None.
thetaDistance.title: Thetadistance

BackendProfile.title: BackendProfile

BenchReport.description: The complete benchmark report payload (the ``BENCH`` object consumed by the web app).

gitBranch.description: Git branch name at benchmark run time. None when git is unavailable. Additive field.
gitBranch.title: Gitbranch

gitCommit.description: Git commit hash (short, ≥7 chars) at benchmark run time. None when git is unavailable or the run is not in a git repo. Additive field — old payloads validate with None.
gitCommit.title: Gitcommit

runTimestampUnix.description: Unix epoch seconds when the benchmark run started (int). None when not captured. Additive field.
runTimestampUnix.title: Runtimestampunix

trustBlock.description: V&V evidence block emitted by oracles.audit.runner. Additive — Pydantic fills None for pre-Plan-E payloads, so old runs still parse.

BenchReport.title: BenchReport

CI.description: A point estimate with its confidence interval.
CI.title: CI

CalibrationResult.description:
Frozen contract view of ``oracles.inference.CalibrationStat``.

Result of a binomial calibration test on parameter pulls
(θ_est − θ_true) / σ_est. All fields mirror ``CalibrationStat`` exactly
so the engine can pass the stat object straight into ``model_validate``.

When fewer than ``InferenceConfig.min_pulls`` pulls were collected, the
engine still emits a **populated** record with ``skipped=True, passed=False,
coverage=0.0, binomial_p=1.0`` — it is never ``None`` for insufficient data.
``None`` on ``InferenceBlock`` only appears for old payloads that predate
the field (additive-minor schema upgrade); the web layer must check
``skipped`` to distinguish "test ran but data was insufficient" from
"old payload that never had the field".

``binomial_p`` is retained as an *honest strict diagnostic*: it reports the
point-null test of H0: coverage = nominal.  At large n this has extreme power
and will reject even a negligible deviation; it is NOT the gate criterion.

``passed`` uses a practical-equivalence rule: the Clopper–Pearson CI must lie
entirely within ``[nominal − equivalence_margin, nominal + equivalence_margin]``.
``equivalence_margin`` carries the pre-registered band (default 0.03 = ±3 pp).
Additive field — old payloads without it default to 0.03 (matching the engine
default) so validation is lossless.
CalibrationResult.title: CalibrationResult

CaseInference.description: Per-case inference: intervals that make each comparison falsifiable.
CaseInference.title: CaseInference

CategoryMeta.description: Benchmark category definition (id, label, case count, hue token).
CategoryMeta.title: CategoryMeta

CredibilityRung.description:
V&V maturity ladder (inspired by ASME V&V credibility levels).

The live wire set earns RUNG_2..RUNG_4. RUNG_1 / RUNG_5 are reserved
end-stops (see the per-member comments), part of the public contract and
pinned by an enum-value test — reserved, not dead.
CredibilityRung.title: CredibilityRung

ExprEdge.description:
One tied/shared-parameter expression edge.

Wire form (camelCase): ``{"targetNode": "p1", "targetParam": "sigma",
"expression": "p0.sigma"}``. Typed (not a bare ``dict[str, str]``) so the
inner keys camelize like every other contract field — the web reads
``edge.targetNode`` / ``edge.targetParam``. Accepts the engine's snake_case
dicts via ``populate_by_name`` on :class:`_Base`.
ExprEdge.title: ExprEdge

Featured.description:
One analyzed case rendered in full detail across all panels.

Per-backend metrics live in one `profiles` map (keyed by solver id); the
remaining fields are case-level (shared across backends). A report carries a
*list* of these (``BenchReport.analyzed``), one per deep-dived case, selectable
in the UI; the `id` is unique within the list.

exprEdges.description: Tied/shared-parameter expression edges (camelCase keys on the wire: targetNode / targetParam / expression). Empty for unconstrained cases. Additive-minor field.
exprEdges.title: Expredges

fixedParams.description: Per-node fixed parameter names, e.g. {"p0": ["center"]} means node p0's center was held at its guess value during the fit. Empty for unconstrained cases. Additive-minor field — old payloads validate with {}.
fixedParams.title: Fixedparams

modelFormula.description: LaTeX formula string for the featured case's model shape. Sourced from PeakModel.formula_latex in the oracle registry. None when no formula is registered. Additive field.
modelFormula.title: Modelformula

modelSourceFile.description: Relative path to the Rust kernel source for this case's model, e.g. "crates/spectrafit-models/src/gaussian.rs". None for landscape/optfn cases without a dedicated kernel. Additive field — old payloads validate with None.
modelSourceFile.title: Modelsourcefile

nestedAdequacy.description: Nested-model adequacy V&V result for this case. Populated when the benchmark engine runs the nested-order oracle (Task 4.3). None for cases without a known generative order or when the oracle was not executed. Additive-minor field — old payloads without nestedAdequacy validate with None.

Featured.title: Featured

GlobalFit.description:
Shared-model multi-spectrum joint global fit (the lmfit ``fit_multi_datasets`` pattern).

**SYNTHETIC placeholder — no experimental data.**  Uses a ``GlobalFitGraph``
fit over synthetic Gaussian peaks; this field is NOT rendered in the production
UI (classified ``ignored: cut``).  Peak centers/widths are shared across every
dataset slice (global analysis) while each slice's amplitudes vary; ``traces``
are the recovered per-peak amplitude traces and ``slices`` carry the per-dataset
observed + jointly-fitted curves on the shared ``x`` grid.

The ``dataset_axis`` / ``axis_label`` names are deliberately axis-neutral: the
global-fit machinery in spectrafit-core (``GlobalFitGraph``) is not specific to
time — here the axis incidentally represents time, but the contract does not
claim that.
GlobalFit.title: GlobalFit

GlobalFitSlice.description:
One dataset slice in a shared-model multi-spectrum global fit: observed + jointly-fitted model.

The ``coord`` field carries the axis coordinate for this slice (e.g. a time
value, a temperature, a sample index) — the name is axis-neutral because the
global-fit machinery is not specific to time.
GlobalFitSlice.title: GlobalFitSlice

InferenceBlock.description:
Top-level inference attached to BenchReport (additive 1.3 -> 1.4).

``calibration`` and ``speed_inference`` are additive-minor fields (Task 5.3):
old payloads that lack them validate with ``None``; the gate axis is inert
when ``None`` (no-pass-by-absence rule).
InferenceBlock.title: InferenceBlock

InferenceConfig.description:
Pre-registered inference parameters (written to the manifest, not tuned).

The four new fields are the Bonferroni-split family α values and the
calibration-test hyper-parameters defined in the cycle 5 global constraints.
They carry defaults so old payloads that lack them validate without a migrator.
InferenceConfig.title: InferenceConfig

ManifestSignals.description:
Headline numbers from ``manifest.json`` surfaced via the contract.

Mirrors the keys :func:`oracles.reports._headline` writes; ``pinned``
is populated from :func:`oracles.reports.read_perf_baseline` (``None``
when no baseline is currently pinned).

gateState.description: Aggregate gate verdict. Single source of truth; the web UI must render this rather than recomputing from regression flags.
gateState.title: Gatestate

harmonicMeanSpeedupVsBaseline.description: Harmonic mean of per-case speedup values vs the baseline solver. Complements the geometric mean per Eeckhout (2024): the harmonic mean is the correct aggregate for equal-time comparisons and is always ≤ the geometric mean for positively-skewed speedup distributions. Additive field — old payloads on disk validate with None.
harmonicMeanSpeedupVsBaseline.title: Harmonicmeanspeedupvsbaseline

nonfiniteDr2CaseIds.description: Case ids whose |Δr²| vs the baseline was non-finite (NaN/Inf). The accuracy gate fails when this is non-empty; carried as a list so _sanitize (which coerces non-finite floats) cannot erase the signal.
nonfiniteDr2CaseIds.title: Nonfinitedr2Caseids

sanitizedValuePaths.description: JSONPath-ish locators ('$.suite[3].m.jax.r2') of non-finite floats the presentation sanitizer coerced to 0.0 when this artifact was written. Non-empty means some rendered 0.0 values are suppressions, not measurements (G5 disclosure, 2026-06-23 tribunal). Carried as a list so _sanitize cannot erase the signal; additive field — old payloads validate with [].
sanitizedValuePaths.title: Sanitizedvaluepaths

saturatedCategories.description: Categories where every supported backend hits r²≥0.999 on every case. Differential below the noise floor; UI should mark these explicitly.
saturatedCategories.title: Saturatedcategories

ManifestSignals.title: ManifestSignals

MultiDim.description:
Genuinely N-dimensional (≥3-D) fit showcase (SP-2).

A synthetic N-D Gaussian is recovered by a real least-squares solve with the
parametric ``gaussian_nd`` kernel (D inferred from the node's indexed
``center_<i>`` parameters). ``peaks`` are the *fitted* parameters and
``r_squared`` the recovery quality. Full N-D obs/model/residual grids are
intentionally NOT stored — they do not scale past 2-D — so ``projections``
carries 2-D marginal slices of the fitted model for any visualization.

**SYNTHETIC — no experimental data; NOT rendered in the production UI
(classified ``ignored: cut``).**
MultiDim.title: MultiDim

NdPeak.description:
A recovered N-D Gaussian peak: amplitude + per-axis center and sigma.

``center`` and ``sigma`` each have ``n_dims`` entries (indexed `center_0..` /
`sigma_0..` from the parametric ``gaussian_nd`` kernel).
NdPeak.title: NdPeak

NestedAdequacy.description:
Frozen contract view of ``oracles.nested.NestedAdequacy``.

Result of a nested-order V&V against a known generative order: checks
that model-selection criteria (LRT / F / AIC / BIC) recover the true
order m* by comparing the (m*−1) reduced, m* true, and (m*+1)
over-fitted models.

Both AIC and BIC verdicts are reported separately.  On the featured
real tri-Gaussian, AIC over-selects the 4-peak model
(``over_not_preferred_aic = False``) while BIC correctly recovers the
true 3-peak order (``over_not_preferred_bic = True``).

``nested_adequacy: NestedAdequacy | None = None`` on ``Featured`` is an
additive-minor field — old payloads that lack it validate with ``None``.
NestedAdequacy.title: NestedAdequacy

NistDataset.description: Per-dataset NIST StRD certified-value validation result.

passed.description: True iff min_sig_figs ≥ the validation threshold.
passed.title: Passed

NistDataset.title: NistDataset

NistParam.description: One parameter's certified-vs-fitted agreement for a NIST StRD dataset.
NistParam.title: NistParam

NistValidation.description:
Aggregate NIST StRD certified-value validation (the W8 evidence block).

Independent external replication against NIST's extended-precision certified
values — the evidence RUNG_5 was reserved for. Additive on TrustBlock.

passed.description: True iff every dataset agrees to ≥ threshold sig figs.
passed.title: Passed

totalAvailable.description: Size of the external NIST StRD nonlinear-regression universe (the denominator for 'N of M' coverage). Emitted by the validation builder so the UI never hardcodes the total. Additive — None for payloads written before this field existed.
totalAvailable.title: Totalavailable

NistValidation.title: NistValidation

PanelLayout.description: Grid sizing intent for one panel — hints, not CSS.
PanelLayout.title: PanelLayout

PanelSpec.description:
One report panel as data: which series, which chart, which slot.

The web app renders these generically (PanelRenderer); adding a panel is
one record in ``oracles.panels.DEFAULT_PANELS``, not a new React block.
``source`` keys into the web-side series-builder registry — it is a contract
string, pinned both sides by the default-panels fixture test.
Additive 1.2 -> 1.3 bump together with ``BenchReport.panels``.
PanelSpec.title: PanelSpec

Peak.description: A single peak contribution curve (featured solver), labeled g1/g2/….
Peak.title: Peak

PeakACS.description: Peak parameters in the report's short form: amplitude / center / sigma.
PeakACS.title: PeakACS

PeakTrace.description: A shared peak's amplitude evolving across the dataset axis.
PeakTrace.title: PeakTrace

PinnedBaseline.description:
The ``.spectrafit_reports/perf_baseline.json`` sidecar surfaced to the web.

Mirrors :func:`oracles.reports.write_perf_baseline`'s on-disk shape so
the gate's self-vs-self signal is one fetch away in the browser.
PinnedBaseline.title: PinnedBaseline

Point2D.description: A single (x, y) point used by line / ecdf / scaling / warmup series.
Point2D.title: Point2D

Projection.description: A 2-D projection (marginal slice) of a higher-D fit: axis-pair + matrix.
Projection.title: Projection

SelectionStats.description:
Frozen contract view of ``oracles.nested.SelectionStats``.

Statistics for one nested-model comparison (full minus reduced):
LRT / F-test / information criteria.  All statistics are "full minus
reduced" oriented: negative Δ-AIC / Δ-BIC means the full model is
preferred.
SelectionStats.title: SelectionStats

SolverFit.description: One solver's fit of the featured case: params + curve + residuals.
SolverFit.title: SolverFit

SolverMeta.description:
Solver legend entry (id, label, and theme color tokens).

Re-exported from oracles.bench_contract for BenchReport.solvers.
SolverMeta.title: SolverMeta

SpeedInferenceResult.description:
Frozen contract view of ``oracles.inference.SpeedStat``.

Result of a geomean-speedup significance test: bootstrap CI on the
geometric mean of per-case speedup ratios plus sign-test and Wilcoxon
signed-rank p-values. All fields mirror ``SpeedStat`` exactly.

When fewer than 2 valid per-case speedups were available, the engine still
emits a **populated** record with ``skipped=True, passed=False,
geomean_speedup=0.0, ci_lo=0.0, ci_hi=0.0`` — it is never ``None`` for
insufficient data. ``None`` on ``InferenceBlock`` only appears for old
payloads that predate the field (additive-minor schema upgrade); the web
layer must check ``skipped`` to distinguish "test ran but data was
insufficient" from "old payload that never had the field".
SpeedInferenceResult.title: SpeedInferenceResult

SpreadPt.description: A mean±sd point versus run count (param-spread / stability series).
SpreadPt.title: SpreadPt

StabilityEntry.description: One backend's metric stability versus run count (mean±sd per metric).
StabilityEntry.title: StabilityEntry

SuiteCase.description: One row in the all-cases suite (regression map / win-rate tables).

winnerReason.description: Short human-readable explanation of WHY the winner won, contrasting key signals (κ(J), n_iter, convergence_efficiency) for the winner vs the baseline. None when the required signals are absent. Additive field — old payloads validate with None.
winnerReason.title: Winnerreason

SuiteCase.title: SuiteCase

SuiteMetric.description: Single-point per-solver metrics for a suite row.

convergenceEfficiency.description: Mean cost reduction per iteration: (cost₀ − costₙ) / n_iter. Populated when a real cost history exists (history_source=='real'); None for reconstructed histories. Additive field — old payloads validate with None.
convergenceEfficiency.title: Convergenceefficiency

illConditioned.description: True when κ(J) ≥ 1e6 (ill-conditioned). None when κ(J) was not computed. Additive field.
illConditioned.title: Illconditioned

metricUndefinedReason.description: Human-readable explanation when red_chi2_weighted is None, e.g. "sigma=0 (noiseless)". Additive field.
metricUndefinedReason.title: Metricundefinedreason

redChi2Weighted.description: σ-weighted reduced-χ²: Σ((y−ŷ)/σ)² / dof. None when σ=0 (noiseless case); see metric_undefined_reason. Additive field — old payloads validate with None.
redChi2Weighted.title: Redchi2Weighted

SuiteMetric.title: SuiteMetric

Summary.description: Headline KPI block per solver for the featured case.
Summary.title: Summary

TimingDist.description: Per-solver runtime distribution over timing repetitions (milliseconds).
TimingDist.title: TimingDist

TrustBlock.description: Aggregate trust evidence attached to a BenchReport.

nistValidation.description: NIST StRD certified-value validation (W8). Additive — Pydantic fills None for payloads that predate the A7 external-validation wire.

TrustBlock.title: TrustBlock

Uncertainty.description:
Parameter-pull diagnostics: pulls = (est−truth)/σ, coverage, reported σ.

``coverage`` is ``None`` when no valid σ was available for any MC sample
(e.g. backends like jax that set every ``param_stderr`` entry to ``None``).
A ``None`` coverage is distinct from a genuine ``0.0`` (all pulls |p| ≥ 1σ).
Uncertainty.title: Uncertainty

Warmup.description: Cold/hot amortization for one solver (JAX compile cost amortizing 1→100).
Warmup.title: Warmup

WarmupPt.description: One cold/hot amortization point: per-run ms at a cumulative run count.
WarmupPt.title: WarmupPt

info.summary: Serves the frozen BenchReport contract (the report the web UI consumes).
info.title: SpectraFit Benchmark API

get.summary: The latest run's report (the BenchReport contract).

get.description: Return the validated :class:`BenchReport` for the given *run_id*.
get.summary: A specific run's report by id.

get.description: Return the run ids under ``<REPORTS_ROOT>/benchmark/`` (newest first).

schema.title: Response List Runs Api Runs Get

get.summary: List available benchmark run ids (newest first).

get.description:
Latest run's verification ledger slice (trust block + inference).

The downloadable 'verification ledger' the UI links to for reviewers. The full
V&V detail (wires, NIST datasets, nested-adequacy, σ-calibration, speed-significance)
lives here rather than as a dedicated UI page; the landing carries only a compact,
externally-anchored trust signal. camelCase wire form, like /report.

schema.title: Response Get Trust Api Trust Get

get.summary: The latest run's verification ledger (trust block + inference).
The latest run's report (the BenchReport contract).

get.description: Return the validated :class:`BenchReport` for the given *run_id*.
get.summary: A specific run's report by id.

get.description: Return the run ids under ``<REPORTS_ROOT>/benchmark/`` (newest first).

schema.title: Response List Runs Api V1 Runs Get

get.summary: List available benchmark run ids (newest first).

get.description:
Latest run's verification ledger slice (trust block + inference).

The downloadable 'verification ledger' the UI links to for reviewers. The full
V&V detail (wires, NIST datasets, nested-adequacy, σ-calibration, speed-significance)
lives here rather than as a dedicated UI page; the landing carries only a compact,
externally-anchored trust signal. camelCase wire form, like /report.

schema.title: Response Get Trust Api V1 Trust Get

get.summary: The latest run's verification ledger (trust block + inference).
